fields.description:
## What
One-time backfill: run the conservative blame -> commit -> ticket resolver over existing closed bugs to draw `caused_by` links retroactively, seeding source-of-bugs history.

## Files
- `scripts/backfill_caused_by.py` — new: iterate closed bugs, reuse `src/rebar/metrics/blame.py`, draw `caused_by` on high-confidence single culprits; `--dry-run` default.
- `tests/scripts/test_backfill_caused_by.py` — new.

## Test tier
The test lives under `tests/scripts/` with `pytestmark = pytest.mark.scripts` (the scripts tier; `tests/unit/scripts/` does NOT exist). External tools are stubbed/mocked so the test is deterministic and needs no scc/lizard/jscpd installed.

## Approach
Reuse the same conservative single-culprit resolver as the live auto-derivation (no new heuristic) so backfilled links are deterministic and consistent with going-forward ones. Idempotent via an EXPLICIT existing-link check: because the low-level `rebar.graph._links._write_link_event` writer has NO built-in idempotency (a closed-bug backfill must write via it, bypassing `add_dependency`'s closed-source guard), the script guards the write itself — before writing it calls `rebar.graph._links._is_active_link(bug_id, culprit_id, "caused_by", tracker_dir)` and SKIPS the bug when an active `caused_by` edge to that culprit already exists. `--dry-run` by default so a run previews before writing; back-out is removing the additive links.

## Tests
- Unit (temp store + git): over a fixture of two closed bugs (one single-culprit, one ambiguous), the script proposes exactly one `caused_by` link; a second run is a no-op.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/scripts/test_backfill_caused_by.py` passes: the script links the single-culprit bug and skips the ambiguous one (`assert links == 1`).
- [ ] re-running is idempotent — a bug already carrying an active `caused_by` to its culprit is skipped (the script checks `_is_active_link` before writing), so a second run draws 0 new links — test asserts this.
